review_title: A Breakthrough in Qualitative Data Coding: ATLAS.ti with AI Enhancement!
description: ATLAS.ti is a game-changer in handling large-scale qualitative data. The integration of AI coding tools catapults the experience into the future, making data analysis not only easier but also more exciting. The support center, too, has been exceptional. Any question I had was met with swift and effective assistance through integrated chat. My experience with ATLAS.ti has been nothing short of thrilling!
author: Eugenija K.
pros: ATLAS.ti takes coding and analysis to a new frontier with its AI coding capabilities. The way it automatically processes my data not only fascinates me but also enhances my workflow. The seamless integration of AI with traditional coding techniques allows me to compare and even blend human intuition with machine intelligence. With features like word clouds and opinion mining, it's exhilarating to see AI technology being applied so innovatively in qualitative coding!
cons: The software already exceeds expectations! Thanks for providing us with these tools and allowing us to focus solely on what really matters - reflexivity!

description: I have experience using several of the top-selling QDA platforms. One of the reasons I am loyal to ATLAS.ti is because its customer service is unsurpassed. It's easy to submit questions about licensing and functions, responses are prompt, and the human beings who send the responses are knowledgeable, reasonable, and kind. They make my life easier!
author: Shannon G.
pros: The user interface is intuitive; running queries is easy.
cons: I've been resisting the subscription format, but it's the common business model these days so I understand. I've been using v.8 for nearly a decade and I'm hoping they've done away with the bundling/unbundling function for v.24!

review_title: Mixed bag, needs a LOT of improvement
description: A mixed bag with great disappointments. The coding feels great and I'm happy about related features, but the cloud, file management, backup, and user interface are horrible. So much potential missed out on essential things that are so standard for modern programmes.
author: Alexander K.
pros: I love that the codes are coloured automatically and show up as such when you go through the document, with the specific code being next to the segment that you have coded it with. I am also a fan of the concept map functionality and how you can just drag codes into it and connect them with each other, and that this creates an actual connection within the code structure, not just in the concept map itself.
cons: Almost everything apart from the coding and concept map feature. I will start with the big issues: file management, the cloud feature, the user interface. File management: The way that you have to organise files is downright horrible and creates unnecessary additional work. There are no folders, so you have to put things together under Groups. If you import a document, it cannot be dragged into the group, but will just be imported without being assigned to anything. When you then want to look for that document, you have to go back to the documents, find it among all of the other ones you have, and then you can drag it into the group that it should be in. It would be so much easier if we just had folders for the documents or if you could drag a document you want to import into the group. Cloud feature: An absolute disaster. Though it is supposed to be a beta version, there is just no transparency on it. No idea where my files are, how to access this cloud (I believe you can't actually access it), and how much space you have left to upload. In addition, saving your project on a cloud service outside Atlas.ti is recommended against, which means that you have to live in constant uncertainty of whether your project stays up-to-date. Cannot really work on different computers, because you have to make sure it's uploaded and since it doesn't do that automatically, if you forget to save and manually upload again, you're screwed. There is no functionality to pull a project from the cloud, so all you can do is upload. User interface: There is just so little transparency about where to go for what you need. For example, if you go into your project, you have a settings button for application preferences, project preferences and Atlas.ti. But, this is not the right options button for when you want to know where this particular project is located. In order to find this, you have to close out of it and look for the settings in the overall project overview. Again, the decision to not put files into folders makes it much more difficult to find the documents you want. I don't really understand the decision to have an explore menu on the left. Much better to just have one consistent way to find your codes, files, etc.

author: Patricio V.
pros: Atlas.ti's ease of use is increbile. It took me literally half an hour to switch from another major CAQDAS to Atlas.ti. The user interface, cross-platform, the exceptional customer service, just everything is far better. There are differences in the methodological approach that have to be considered when switching to Atlas.ti. For example, the node hierarchy works differently. And so too does the classification of cases (persons, organizations) have its particularities in Atlas.ti. But none of the necessary adaptations represent a major issue in switching. Beyond that, Atlast.ti does a much better job in every other aspect of qualitative data analysis.
cons: Case classifications Node hierarchy (impossibility to use parent nodes for coding)
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The ease of use (with no training), the smoothness of the interface and the extremely responsive and knowledgeable support team (responded within seconds at 8pm).
cons: Finding highly specific functions was a little fiddly, (I had two transcripts connected to a single audio track and was unable to delete one until I had disconnected them - a highly specific senario which was easily solved with the very responsive technical support team!)
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: I'm satisfied! It's much better than the cumbersome Nvivo. Coding is really easy, and it offers lots of other features that I haven't used yet, but which don't clutter up the interface for simpler tasks. So I think it's a pretty solid and simpler tool, even though it offers many options for exploring data.
author: Andrea B.
pros: Atlas.TI is a robust application for qualitative research. I like how light and user-friendly the interface is. It's easy to learn how to use! Researchers can collaborate online (although there is still room for improvement in this area).
cons: The transition from computer to cloud is not easy. There is a 500 MB limit for importing projects from the desktop version to the cloud version. However, you will only find this out after you try to do so. It is not particularly emphasized when you start working with the desktop version.

review_title: What Atlas should be, but is actually not.
description: Terrible, frustrating, slow, hiccup, non customizable, non field tested, fast coded (not compact) program, that sabotages your work, workflow, for exactly that basic elemental work you have to do in social qualitative research: transcribing and coding. The fundamental, hiccups, no customization is counter productive and especially sabotages your work(flow). It's utterly terrible.
author: Billie V.
pros: I don't know, maybe the analysing after coding? The visualization, what makes it good for research. Maybe the automatic labels you can create and don't have to manually write (in the old days?). Nothing more.
cons: It is the least customizable program in history. - the constant saving when you apply a code, why can't I change that? For every code I apply, I have to wait for 10 -15 seconds for the project to save. Every time! It's sabotaging my process. - The hotkeys, you cannot change hotkeys. Why? And who was the genius to program this in a way that when you transcribe, the spacebar is the pause button? When I transcribe, this very slow hiccup program, sometimes get's out of edit mode. So when you type, and push spacebar, the video recording starts to play, pauses, starts to play again, pauses. Sometimes, due to other hotkeys, the sound is muted, or you start at the beginning of the video. - Applying codes, takes a long time to process. - Constant hiccups. Slow, counter productive for research, and as a student, it really frustrates all these hiccups. At the end of the day, maybe 1 or 2 hours are basically lost due to this programs hiccups, constant saving and what not.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: ATLAS.ti intuitive interface enabled rapid coding and analysis of large volumes of transcribed interviews. The ability to organize and visualize data through concept maps facilitated the identification of emerging patterns and themes. Furthermore, the integration of various types of data, such as texts, videos and audios, enriched the analysis. Real-time collaboration with colleagues through the web platform made teamwork efficient and fluid.
author: Reylis Michel E.
pros: ATLAS.Ti, the latest version of its web and desktop software, offers numerous benefits for analyzing qualitative data. With the web version, projects can be accessed and worked on from anywhere in the world, making it easy to collaborate with people from different time zones and locations. This feature is perfect for working together on research projects, such as studying business sustainability. Plus, the web version ensures that everyone is using the latest version of the software, so there are no issues with different versions not working together. The desktop version is like a powerhouse, capable of handling large tasks and processing tons of data without needing the internet to speed things up. It's like having a powerful tool that allows you to dig deeper into the data and discover hidden patterns and insights. Both versions are like two sides of the same coin, allowing researchers to move projects between them seamlessly. It's like having the best of both worlds, with the flexibility to work in different environments and adapt the analysis to the specific requirements of the project.
cons: ATLAS.ti is an excellent qualitative analysis platform.

description: It was very positive overall. It took some time to sort out the tech issues, but once this was done and I got the hang of things, it has been very useful for my data analysis. Service support was outstanding.
author: G P.
pros: Powerful tool for analysis, which is user-friendly
cons: I experienced frustrating problems with set up - though these do not to seem to have been Atlas' fault. The user manual wasn't entirely clear,
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: A useful tool for comparative qualitative analysis
author: Leiza B.
pros: Customer service is amazing- they reply very fast and give very useful -and patient- guidance
cons: I found some tools a bit more complex and less intuitive than before- such as exporting the number of quotes per group of documents and codes

description: Good, very easy to navigate, and with the undo button its really easy to play around with some functions.
author: Mamello K.
pros: It makes it to analyse the qualitative data, and i can always go back and change code, or merge or split, its quite helpful.
cons: Mine crashed, and i am not sure if there ia some information on how to go about that in the manual because I was just frustrated, because i could still open projects but the documents content was not showing in the main window, and i could not creat new project.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: It is a software that requires time and testing exercises to understand how each of the tools works, but once this process is done you can achieve great mastery of its use. You also have personalized advice 24 hours a day by chat or zoom
author: Sophia G.
pros: Virtual advice 24 hours a day that provides the tool to resolve doubts or concerns in the use of the software, in several languages
cons: The web version has many analysis tools in beta mode, which makes it difficult to work synchronously in a collaborative group. Therefore, it is necessary to work on the computer version to get the most out of the AI tools that have recently been incorporated.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: A challenging part of working in a team is keeping track of and putting together everyone’s work. With ATLAS.ti, we were able to easily handle this challenge and spend more time exploring each person’s codings and producing impacting findings.
author: Roberto B.
pros: Each person can work on coding at the same time, and after merging projects the codings can be compared. The intercoder agreement tool provides a great visualization to easily explore any part of the coded data, and it calculates different measures of agreement.
cons: My team uses the ICA tool a lot, but sometimes it does not work for me. I already received help from the technicians though, and they were fast and helpful.

review_title: A great contribution to qualitative research
description: In general it is a tool that I have found very useful in qualitative research with large numbers of data and with research teams and networks developing analyses simultaneously.
author: José R.
pros: It is a very useful tool when working with large amounts of data. It provides very good assistance for the systematization of analysis and collaborative work. It also provides useful outputs to continue with quantitative analysis, and visuals for the representation of the results.
cons: There is still room for improvement in user friendliness, visualization and integration of AI tools. Also in maintaining a price range more suitable for non-commercial uses, e.g. educational and social research. In terms of data, further development of audiovisual data analysis would be interesting.

review_title: Excellent tool ~ brilliant across various data types and sources
author: Carlien K.
pros: There are incredible aspects of the programme (all round). The ease of using the programme intuitively (both Windows and Mac versions), as well as the various entities (codes, memos, groups, commenting functions, etc.) that you can choose how you want to apply it. You can create your own system of when to use a comment on a specific entity in a particular way. The flexibility within the programme is wonderful considering that computerised programmes often have one approach to which you can apply content. In other words, you can personalise your analysis approach using comments and memos to keep track of your individual approach to applying the entities. As a predominantly visual person, I absolutely love the network views that you can generate when considering how your coded data interacts. You are in control of the experience and must be mindful to generate your relational understandings as embedded in your data. When you conduct your analysis systematically and build up your smaller code-code relationships, the networks become a powerful way to visualise your abstract thoughts and ideas to communicate and disseminate data well.
cons: When training others to use the programme, a rookie mistake is thinking that the programme will do the analysis for researchers. It's a tough barrier to sometimes break when teaching others how to use the programme, but once people realise that your qualitative analysis skills run alongside the programme tools, the potential is incredible. It takes a lot of skill outside of the programme to conduct GREAT analyses inside the programme.
author_profession: Entrepreneur, adventurer and possibly a qualitative research ninja

review_title: Great qualitative data analysis tool for novice and professional!
description: The software has a user-friendly interface and intuitive design, making the process much faster and easier for me. Atlas.ti has been extremely beneficial for my mixed method approach. Its table matrix tool made it simple for me to integrate the results from my qualitative phase, which in turn allowed me to develop my questionnaire items in a clear and organized manner. This ultimately resulted in a more efficient and effective data analysis process.I also appreciate the attractive design of the software, which made my experience more enjoyable. I have recommended it to my students as a solid choice for qualitative data analysis too. The support provided by Atlas.to trainers and consultants is also a good added value.
author: Anis Najiha A.
pros: What I liked most about Atlas.ti was its user-friendly interface and intuitive design, which made my data analysis process much more efficient and enjoyable. The software's table matrix tool was also one of the features that I really like, as it allowed me to integrate my qualitative findings and develop my questionnaire items in a clear and organized manner.
cons: One potential drawback of Atlas.ti is its new pricing system, which some users may find expensive. There is no no lifetime license but 3 years lease , although it is to make sure the software is update and continuous support.

description: As an ATLAS.ti user who uses the tool often in his professional work, and also in the academic field, I consider it to be one of the most valuable tools for data analysis. I have a high level of satisfaction with this software, with the support of the ATLAS.ti academy and its customer service department.
author: Marvin R.
pros: By way of context, I can mention that I am an experienced user of ATLAS.ti, with more than 10 years using the tool, and teaching and advising researchers from very different disciplines on how to perform data analysis with the support of software. This experience allows me to affirm that version 23 of ATLAS.ti represents one of the most important and innovative advances in software for qualitative data analysis. Before version 23, ATLAS.ti was the leading tool in data analysis due to its intuitive interface, the integration of different data formats in the same project, and the data visualization possibilities. Version 23 made an important leap in at least three aspects. First, the incorporation of automatic coding tools and preparation of data summaries using artificial intelligence. I consider that this is a substantial change in this version, and that it represents valuable support for the analyst. Second, interoperability between the different versions of ATLAS.ti (MAC, Windows, IOS, Android and Web) has improved significantly. The versions are now more similar, and the ease of sharing projects between different platforms has improved substantially. Third, we are at a time of prolific development of artificial intelligence tools and ATLAS.ti has put two into production this year, and it is highly likely that in the coming months we will see new developments, which will facilitate and enhance data analysis. Finally, I must mention that one of the things I like most about ATLAS.ti is the positive impression it generates on students and researchers who have never used data analysis software, and when they see a demonstration or listen to a class, they get excited due to the characteristics and robustness of ATLAS.ti, which motivates them to learn and use it in their own research. This is an impression that has been constant in my ten years as a research teacher.
cons: I think one of the aspects of improvement in ATLAS.ti is networking. Usually, when a code is networked with its citations, the citations cannot be seen completely. Adding this functionality as optional could allow you to explore the data in greater detail using networks. However, creating the networks and exporting them in graphic format is very simple.

description: I have been using ATLAS.ti for the past 6 years and I really enjoyed it than any other qualitative analysis software I have ever used.
author: Victor I.
pros: ATLAS.ti is a software with excellent graphic view which makes it possible to view major major issues in a set of data at a glance. Features such as Sankey diagram, networks, code tree, code forest etc. makes it possible to get a whole lot of information about a data set without necessarily read through the data. It also provides information on frequency of each item which gives reader an idea of prevalence or importance (number of times such issue was referred to in the data) certain items listed in those diagram in the data set. The software also allows one to change colour of codes which resulted in a beautiful and presentable diagram. These diagrams are easy to generate and manipulate to the form in which you want it presented. Using these diagrams had added professionalism to my qualitative reports and clients found it informative, illustrative and attractive.
cons: Talking about graphics in ATLAS.ti, what I don't really like, therefore, don't use is the bar chart because it tends to be too scattered by presenting document or document groups individually and it doesn't label the bars with the codes they represent neither is there colour kay to provide more clarification to the reader.

author: Wahid B.
pros: For the past two years, ATLAS.ti has been our preferred tool for qualitative research. The platform excels at accommodating various types of data, providing the versatility our different projects require. Its user-friendly workspace is key to enhancing the quality of our research, allowing easy customization for each project's specific needs. AI-driven features further boost our analytical process with smart coding suggestions. When it comes to teamwork, the platform's smooth collaboration features make it an ideal choice for research groups focused on both quality and efficiency.
cons: So far, the software has consistently surpassed my expectations without presenting any drawbacks.

review_title: User friendly and helpful support team
description: If you have lots of text and data to deal with, the software can help you to summarize and find the ideas and meanings from the massive data much more easily.
author: Peying F.
pros: Very easy to code, group, and analyze all my interview transcripts and other texts. The function is intuitional so that you can familiarize yourself with the coding and analyzing steps easily. Once you have any problem with the software, the online message and service mail from the support team is really helpful and instant-reply.
cons: New function-AI summary can do the summary only from the text and data, just to make the massive data short, but if you want to "read behind the data", we have the sensitivity of the issue and that's our job that AI still can not replace.

author: Hilmi T.
pros: ATLAS.ti have the features to analyze, find sentiment, concept and opinion in organizing a code. We also can find the input by reading the article ourselves but the management (code, document, memos, networks) really helps us understand what we read before.
cons: maybe they can release the ATLAS.ti for phone apps

description: i’ve done my qualitative research using this software and it helped me a lot with the data analysis. it worth the money especially if you’re a student and get this software through a student discount.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: this software enables the users to easily codes and analyze qualitative datas, such as interview transcripts and documents. the interface is user-friendly and it offers student discount at an affordable price compared to its competitors. the free trial version also doesn’t have time-limit, instead, it limits the document and codes but you can keep the free trial as long as you like.
cons: the cloud version is not as responsive as the pc/mac version, it also would be better if the pc/mac version has an option to save the project to cloud.

review_title: Worst customer support I've ever experienced!
author: Stijn W.
pros: Good functionality, not too hard to use, although more advanced functionality is harder to find out and learn to use. Analysis of data could be more extensive.
cons: After doing an update of Atlas.ti 8 to the newest version (19 I think it was), I experienced some problems in the document view. About 80% of the quotations that I had made and the codes that were connected to these just disappeared directly after installation of the update. I reported the problem through the Problem Report option within Atlas, I called telephone support, I emailed the technical support team and, upon their request, I send them a Project Bundle to look into. Over the last two weeks, I have been calling 5 or 6 times to telephone support and sent multiple emails. The only response I got from telephone support (very friendly, although not able to help) was that they would pass on my request/complaints, as they couldn't do any more. I got two emails back from the technical support team in the first few days: one asking to report the problem within Atlas (which I already did and then did a second time) and to send a project bundle, the second email made apologies for waiting, confirmed receiving my project bundle and problem report and promised to get back within "a few days". That was 10 days ago. I have been waiting for two weeks now, even as it seems that this is not a very hard problem to solve (the quotations and codes are still there, no data seems to be lost), as it is just a problem in the document view. Moreover, the problem occurred directly after updating to a new version of Atlas, so literally nothing was changed in my own setting. Terrible support!!
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Lara G.
pros: I recently contacted the support team of Atlas.ti, as I had a problem when upgrading the program. My concerns were listened carefully, and my problem got solved in a friendly and professional manner. I was extremely satisfied after the experience! Many thanks to [sensitive content hidden] (from the support team) for all her patience, help, and assistance.
cons: My projects were not transferred automatically to the new version of Atlas.ti when upgrading the program, which was quite scary. However, after contacting the support team, I got all my projects and work back quite easily.

description: ATLAS.ti helps me to organize pieces of information from any kind of document. And in ATLAS.ti I can read this and make my analyses properly. I fell that my performance as a researcher has increased after starting to use ATLAS.ti.
author: Murilo S.
pros: It is a fantastic software for any research. For example, I use this for my literature reviews and can better manage the information I would like to use in my reports. It also has the possibility of many different visualizations. I enjoy the facility to create a visual network from textual quotations or figures.
cons: To be honest, I have difficulty saying something that I dislike. But, for sure, they could make some improvements there, although the ATLAS.ti is updated time by time and we can suggest improvements as well.
